turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I feel like I'm always working. Even when I try to take a break, my mind keeps going back to work. How can I manage this better?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: That sounds really tough. Have you tried setting specific times for checking emails and other work-related tasks? It might help to create some clear boundaries.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I've tried that, but it's hard because my job is always demanding. I feel like I'm never doing enough.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: It can be really challenging when your work feels all-consuming. Maybe try to focus on one task at a time and give yourself permission to take breaks. That might help you stay more focused during work hours.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: That's a good idea, but I'm worried that if I take too many breaks or don't work as much, my boss will think I'm not committed enough.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Understanding your manager's perspective is important. You could try having an open conversation about your workload and how you manage your time. Sometimes they might be more understanding than you think once you explain the situation clearly.
